note: BRAIN layer — Day 47 (Thursday 2026-05-14). Eve of Batch 5 Day-3. This file contains Day-3 bump copy ready for HANDS to send tomorrow (Friday 2026-05-15, 09:00-10:30). No per-lead data file received from HANDS — copy provided as per-signal templates. HANDS populates [bracketed placeholders] from their send records. Thread-reply to original Day-0 email. 3-4 lines max. Signal-specific — must reference the exact finding from Day-0.
new_sends_note: No new initial sends today (Thursday). No new cohort ready. Batch 5 is Day-3 tomorrow.

day3_bumps_tomorrow.type: Thread reply to original Day-0 email
day3_bumps_tomorrow.imap_precondition: MANDATORY per lead before each send. If lead has replied already: see conditional handling below.
rules: Reply to the original email thread — do NOT send a new standalone email
Reference the specific signal from Day-0 exactly once — no new pitch
No question marks in the subject (inherited from thread — no subject to set)
3-4 lines only — short bump. Not a restatement of the full email.
First name only — no 'Hi [first name]' opener needed (conversational thread reply)
No pressure language. No 'just checking in' — be specific.
Do NOT send if IMAP reveals lead has replied — see conditional_handling below.

if_imap_finds_interested_reply_before_bu.action: Still send Day-3 bump — it arrives as a follow-up in the thread and reinforces responsiveness
if_imap_finds_interested_reply_before_bu.note: Write reply to new_replies.json immediately. Classify. Prepare proposal within 24 hours. Activate LinkedIn Day 7 hard deadline (2026-05-19).
if_imap_finds_interested_reply_before_bu.bump_text_adjustment: Use the 'interested variant' — acknowledge their reply positively, offer next step.

if_imap_finds_not_interested_reply.action: CANCEL bump. Block email channel permanently. Block LinkedIn. Log CHANGELOG.

if_imap_finds_unsubscribe.action: CANCEL bump. Suppress within 24 hours (GDPR Article 21). Log audit_log and CHANGELOG.

if_imap_finds_ooo.action: PAUSE sequence. Note return date. Resume Day-3 bump day after stated return. Do not send during stated OOO period.

if_imap_finds_wrong_person.action: Cancel sequence for this email. Attempt to find correct contact via website or Companies House. Log CHANGELOG.

ssl_pas_day3.subject: [Inherited from thread — do not change]
ssl_pas_day3.body:
[First name],

Just wanted to make sure this landed — the 'Not Secure' warning on [specific URL from Day-0] is still showing.

Happy to talk through the fix if useful.

[Your name]
personalisation_required: specific URL (from send records — the exact URL used in Day-0 email)
rules: Use the exact URL you referenced in Day-0 — builds verification credibility
Do NOT add new pain points — this is a bump only
'Not Secure' in single quotes — Chrome's exact wording, same as Day-0
3 lines body. One reference to the signal. One offer. No more.
ssl_pas_day3.confirmed_winner_pattern: SSL-PAS produced 3/3 INTERESTED leads in campaign — this bump reinforces the same signal

mobile_pas_day3.subject: [Inherited from thread — do not change]
mobile_pas_day3.body:
[First name],

Just checking this got through — [business name]'s site is still not loading properly on a mobile screen.

Worth a quick look if you've got 10 minutes.

[Your name]
rules: Reference 'mobile screen' — same language as Day-0
Do NOT add SSL or any second signal — mobile bump only
'10 minutes' framing is low-pressure — keeps it light
No question mark needed — statement with soft call to action works better for trades

no_website_bab_day3.send_type: Thread reply (or new email if original had no reply-thread — check send records)
no_website_bab_day3.subject: [Inherited from thread if exists — otherwise: 'Following up — [Business name], [town]']
no_website_bab_day3.body:
Hi,

Following up on my note from a few days ago — when I search for '[trade] [town]' on Google, [business name] still isn't coming up.

Competitors with websites are getting those calls instead. Worth 10 minutes to chat?

[Your name]
rules: If owner name not known: use 'Hi' — do not guess
Reference the specific search term used in Day-0 if known
'Competitors with websites are getting those calls' is the BAB contrast — keep it
No technical language — these are offline businesses

aida_keyword_gap_day3.subject: [Inherited from thread — do not change]
aida_keyword_gap_day3.body:
[First name],

Quick follow-up — [business name] has [N] reviews but '[keyword] [town]' still isn't surfacing the website in Google.

Worth a look?

[Your name]
rules: Use the exact review count from Day-0 — credibility anchor
Use the exact keyword from Day-0
Short — high-review leads get many approaches. Shorter is better.
No pressure. 'Worth a look?' is low-friction for established businesses.

interested_reply_variant.note: If IMAP finds an INTERESTED reply before Day-3 bump fires: use this variant instead of the standard bump. Reply to the thread. Acknowledge their reply. Move to next step.
interested_reply_variant.ssl_interested_variant:
[First name],

Great — thanks for coming back to me.

Happy to pull together a few options and talk through what makes sense for [business name]. Are you free for a quick call this week?

[Your name]
interested_reply_variant.no_website_interested_variant:
Hi,

Good to hear from you — thanks for getting back to me.

I can put together a few options for getting [business name] visible online. Are you free for a quick call this week?

[Your name]
rules: Acknowledge promptly — do not re-pitch
Offer a call — don't ask for more information over email
Match tone to their reply — mirror formality level
Log in CHANGELOG same session

send_brief_for_hands.instruction: For each lead in your Batch 5 send records, apply the correct template from copy_by_signal above. Fill all [bracketed placeholders] from your send records. Do NOT use templates directly — personalise every entry. Send as thread reply to original email. 10-minute stagger. IMAP clean per lead required before each send.
send_brief_for_hands.total_expected_sends: 12-18 (depends on confirmed send list from Tuesday 2026-05-12)
after_sends: Log all sends to CHANGELOG.md same session (47 days overdue — mandatory)
Execute LinkedIn connection requests (1-hour gap after last email bump)
Write data/linkedin_profiles.json (mandatory — one entry per lead searched)

batch_5_copy_templates_legacy.status: Day-0 templates from Day 44/45 runs. Carried forward for reference. Day-3 bump copy above supersedes for tomorrow's sends.
batch_5_copy_templates_legacy.note: Original Day-0 template copy is in the Day 44 version of this file. If needed for reference: check git history or the Day-44 BRAIN run outputs.

send_summary.copy_status: Ready — per-signal templates above. HANDS fills placeholders from send records.
send_summary.imap_precondition: MANDATORY per lead before each bump
send_summary.linkedin_same_day: Yes — after bumps. 1-hour gap. See linkedin_search_plan.json.
